summary: The Czech Republic's posture is set by the National Cyber and Information Security Agency, which explains the quantum threat and quantum-resistant cryptography in an appendix to its minimum requirements and sets out expectations for when post-quantum cryptography should be deployed for sensitive information of critical confidentiality in higher-risk environments. The agency's portal now supports quantum-resistant cryptography, and its National Cyber Security Strategy 2026-2030 names post-quantum cryptography among the technologies for strengthening cyber security. No specific algorithm list or dated national migration deadline has been published.
govActors:
NÚKIB | National Cyber and Information Security Agency, national authority for cyber security
ČNB | Czech National Bank, central bank research on quantum-resistant signature schemes

EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
Model 2026-06-14 (EU / taker, High): EU coordinated roadmap (NIS Cooperation Group); adopts the NIST baseline as a taker. No national algorithm.

obligation: Essential and important entities must use state-of-the-art cryptography under NIS2; migration follows the EU coordinated 2026/2030/2035 timeline. No distinct national post-quantum instrument is recorded in the Atlas sources.
analyticalNote: Czechia is bound by the EU regulatory spine (NIS2, DORA) and follows the coordinated roadmap; no national algorithm or dedicated post-quantum statute is recorded, so it adopts the NIST baseline under EU coordination. Watch point: national transposition and any country-specific guidance would refine this EU-floor entry.
